title: pentacalia albotecta
intro: Pentacalia albotecta is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, also known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, where it grows in the wild as a shrub or small tree, producing small white flowers and edible leaves. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of Pentacalia albotecta have been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves can be prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping them in hot water, or used in herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
